title: ClassificationRecipe
description:
Train a supervised classifier on labeled reference points; apply pixel-by-pixel to one or more input images for a categorical land-cover map (+ optional per-class probabilities + regression image).

Pipeline: stack input images + optional auxiliary imagery → sample features at training points → train classifier → apply per pixel.

Bands: always `class` (uint8). For RANDOM_FOREST/GRADIENT_TREE_BOOST/CART/SVM/NAIVE_BAYES: `class_probability` (0–100) + `probability_<value>` per class. For RF/GBT/CART only: `regression` (continuous; useful when class values ordinal).

AOI implicit (input imagery geometry).

scale.description: Optional per-pixel processing scale (m). Omitted → uses input's native scale (Landsat 30, S2 10). Higher = coarser/cheaper; lower → native resolution.

inputImagery.title: ClassificationInputImagery
inputImagery.description: Source images providing per-pixel features. ≥1 required; multiple stack band-wise (classifier sees union; duplicate band names — later overrides earlier).

inputImage.title: ClassificationInputImage
inputImage.description: Single input. `bandSetSpecs` controls which bands (raw + derived) reach the classifier.

imageId.description: UUID for this entry (React key + panel reference). NOT the data-source id — that's `id`.

type.description: `RECIPE_REF` = SEPAL recipe id (mosaic, time-series, etc.); `ASSET` = EE FeatureCollection/Image/ImageCollection asset id.

id.description: Recipe id or EE asset id (per `type`).

bands.description: All bands the source exposes. GUI-populated. Classifier's actual feature set comes from `bandSetSpecs.included` + derived. LLM only sets when constructing from scratch with known bands.

bandSetSpecs.description:
Specs for which bands (raw + derived) to expose to the classifier. Three types:
- `IMAGE_BANDS` — direct band selection. `included` = band names to keep.
- `PAIR_WISE_EXPRESSION` — pairwise band combinations under one `operation` (RATIO, NORMALIZED_DIFFERENCE, DIFFERENCE, DISTANCE = atan2/π, ANGLE = hypot). `included` = source band names; classifier sees one feature per pair.
- `INDEXES` — spectral indexes (NDVI, EVI, etc.). `included` = index names.

operation.description: Required for `PAIR_WISE_EXPRESSION`. Applied to each ordered band pair.

included.description: Source band names (IMAGE_BANDS / PAIR_WISE_EXPRESSION) or index names (INDEXES).

legend.title: ClassificationLegend
legend.description: Class labels the classifier predicts. Every distinct integer in training data must correspond to one entry; entries sorted by `value` before training. ≥2 entries needed for meaningful classification.

value.description: Class id in `class` band. Unique across entries.

trainingData.title: ClassificationTrainingData
trainingData.description:
Sources of `(point, class)` reference data; recipe samples each, joins, feeds to classifier. ≥1 set; multiple unioned.

Each entry has type-specific fields (CSV/EE-table refs, sampling params, column mappings); GUI populates via wizard. **LLM should default to a single `type='COLLECTED'` entry with empty `referenceData`** — user populates interactively.

trainingDataSet.title: ClassificationTrainingDataSet
trainingDataSet.description: Single reference-data source. Fields beyond the discriminator depend on `type`; `additionalProperties: true` because the GUI wizard model is intricate and version-prone.

type.description:
Source.
- `COLLECTED` — points clicked via GUI collect panel; stored in `referenceData`. Default starting point.
- `RECIPE` — points from another SEPAL recipe (typically another classification used as labeled ground truth). Requires `recipe` id.
- `EE_TABLE` — points from EE FeatureCollection asset. Requires `eeTable`, location columns, class column mapping.
- `CSV_UPLOAD` — uploaded CSV. Requires column mappings.
- `SAMPLE_CLASSIFICATION` — sampled from existing classification (asset/recipe). Requires `samplesPerClass`, `sampleScale`.
- `CEO` — Collect Earth Online project import. Requires institution/project ids.

referenceData.description: Collected reference points (primarily `type='COLLECTED'`). Each point: `x`/`y` in WGS84, integer `class`.

auxiliaryImagery.title: ClassificationAuxiliaryImagery
auxiliaryImagery.description:
Optional auxiliary feature layers added to every input pixel.
- `LATITUDE` — pixel latitude (degrees). Useful when class boundaries vary with latitude (climate zones, biomes).
- `TERRAIN` — elevation, slope, aspect, eastness/northness from SRTM 30 m. **Strongly recommended in mountainous regions.**
- `WATER` — JRC Global Surface Water (occurrence, change, seasonality, max-extent, transition masks). For water-related classifications.

Default `[]`. Each adds compute cost — pick only what's relevant.

classifier.title: ClassificationClassifier
classifier.description:
Algorithm + hyperparameters. `type` discriminates 7 algorithms; each has its own required params (rules.js enforces); ignores others. Defaults provided so switching `type` doesn't require respecifying.

- `RANDOM_FOREST` (default) — robust, non-linear, low tuning. Default for most classifications. Supports class probability + regression.
- `GRADIENT_TREE_BOOST` — sequential boosting; can outperform RF with more tuning. Same outputs as RF.
- `CART` — single decision tree; fast, interpretable; prone to overfit. Probability + regression.
- `NAIVE_BAYES` — feature-independence assumption; cheap, often competitive baseline. Probability only.
- `SVM` — libsvm. Sub-modes (C-SVC, ν-SVC, one-class) and kernels (linear/poly/rbf/sigmoid). **Sensitive to feature scaling — `normalize='YES'` strongly recommended.**
- `MINIMUM_DISTANCE` — nearest-class-mean. Trivially fast; usually a baseline. Benefits from normalization.
- `DECISION_TREE` — apply pre-built EE-format tree (or ensemble JSON). No training step.

numberOfTrees.description: Trees in forest. Used by RF + GBT. More = smoother surface, linear cost. Default 25; production maps often use several hundred.

variablesPerSplit.description: Variables sampled per split (mtry). RF only. `null` (default) → EE picks √(numFeatures). Setting explicitly is unusual; lower = more tree diversity, less per-tree accuracy.

minLeafPopulation.description: Min training samples per leaf. RF + CART. Higher = more regularization (less overfit, more bias). Default 1.

bagFraction.description: Bag fraction per tree. RF only. Default 0.5. Lower = more tree diversity (better OOB estimates); higher = more data per tree.

maxNodes.description: Hard cap on nodes per tree. RF + GBT + CART. `null` (default) = unbounded (limited only by `minLeafPopulation`). Regularization tool when trees overfit.

seed.description: Random seed. RF + GBT. Default 1. Change to compare independent runs; keep fixed for reproducible maps.

shrinkage.description: GBT learning rate. Default 0.005. Smaller = more stable, needs more trees; larger = faster, risks overshoot. Typical 0.001–0.1.

samplingRate.description: GBT per-tree subsample rate. Default 0.7. <1 introduces randomness across trees; 1 = deterministic full-data trees.

loss.description: GBT loss. `LeastAbsoluteDeviation` (default) = robust to outliers. `LeastSquares` textbook. `Huber` between.

lambda.description: Categorical NB likelihood smoothing. Default 0.000001 (essentially un-smoothed). Higher = more regularization, biases toward prior.

decisionProcedure.description: SVM multiclass aggregation. `Voting` (default) = pairwise vote; `Margin` = largest decision margin.

svmType.description:
libsvm variant.
- `C_SVC` (default) — C-parameterized soft-margin classification.
- `NU_SVC` — ν-parameterized; ν bounds margin-error fraction and SV fraction.
- `ONE_CLASS` — one-class novelty detection. Valid only when legend has exactly 2 classes (one positive, one negative); requires `oneClass` to identify positive.

kernelType.description: SVM kernel. `LINEAR` (default) simplest/fastest; `RBF` typical non-linear choice; `POLY`/`SIGMOID` specialized.

shrinking.description: libsvm shrinking heuristic. Default true (faster training, same result).

degree.description: POLY kernel degree. Default 3. Higher = more complex boundaries, more training time, overfit risk.

gamma.description: Kernel coefficient (POLY/RBF/SIGMOID). `null` (default) → EE picks `1/numFeatures`. Smaller γ = smoother decision surface.

coef0.description: Kernel independent term (POLY/SIGMOID). Default 0. Modulates higher-degree terms in POLY and SIGMOID activation midpoint.

cost.description: Soft-margin penalty C (`svmType='C_SVC'`). Default 1. Higher = penalize misclassification more; lower = more slack.

nu.description: ν parameter (`svmType='NU_SVC'`). Default 0.5. Upper bound on margin-error fraction, lower bound on SV fraction.

oneClass.description: Positive class value for `svmType='ONE_CLASS'`. Must match one of the two `legend.entries[*].value`.

metric.description: MINIMUM_DISTANCE metric. Default `euclidean`. `cosine` = angle-only; `mahalanobis` accounts for class-internal covariance. (Legacy schema also listed `manhattan` — EE backend doesn't support it.)

decisionTree.description: EE-serialized decision tree, OR JSON-array of multiple trees (ensemble). DECISION_TREE only — no training; tree applied directly. Typically pasted from `.explain()` output or hand-authored ruleset.

tileScale.description: EE reducer tile-scale multiplier. Used by all training-based classifiers (not DECISION_TREE); especially relevant for SVM + MINIMUM_DISTANCE (normalization runs `reduceRegion`). Default 1. Increase (2/4/8) to avoid OOM on large AOIs (more compute time).

normalize.description: Per-band unit-scale before training. SVM + MINIMUM_DISTANCE only — both feature-scale-sensitive. Default `YES`. `NO` only if inputs already on comparable scale.

allOf.description: SVM and MINIMUM_DISTANCE require tileScale and normalize.
